title: Protocols for genomic epidemiology and source attribution of enteric bacteria causing diarrhoeal disease across Africa
authors: Badji H, Pascoe B, et al.
summary: A shared framework for coordinated One Health sampling, microbiology, sequencing and analysis across African study settings.

title: Recent emergence of a multidrug-resistant Campylobacter coli lineage linked to poultry intensification in the Peruvian Amazon
authors: Pascoe B, Mourkas E, Schiaffino F, et al.
summary: Population-genomic evidence connecting an emerging C. coli lineage, antimicrobial resistance and changing poultry systems.

title: A stable, hierarchical LIN code system for Campylobacter jejuni and Campylobacter coli
authors: Parfitt KM, Pascoe B, Jolley KA, et al.
summary: A stable genomic naming framework designed to make Campylobacter populations easier to compare across datasets and time.

title: Phenotypic resistance of ciprofloxacin- and azithromycin-resistant Campylobacter isolates to an extended antibiotic panel
authors: Romaina-Cachique L, Schiaffino F, et al.
summary: Extended phenotypic characterisation of resistant Campylobacter isolates from the Peruvian Amazon.

title: Sharing of cmeRABC alleles between C. coli and C. jejuni associated with extensive drug resistance
authors: Cooper KK, Mourkas E, Schiaffino F, et al.
summary: Interspecies genetic exchange contributes to extensive drug resistance in infant and poultry isolates.

title: Machine learning to attribute the source of Campylobacter infections in the United States
authors: Pascoe B, Futcher G, Pensar J, et al.
summary: National genomic surveillance used to estimate the contributions of poultry, cattle and other reservoirs.

title: The epidemiology and impact of persistent Campylobacter infections on childhood growth
authors: Schiaffino F, Colston JM, et al.
summary: A multi-country analysis placing persistent Campylobacter detection in the context of childhood growth.

title: Proximity to humans is associated with antimicrobial-resistant enteric pathogens in wild-bird microbiomes
authors: Mourkas E, Valdebenito JO, et al.
summary: Human-altered ecology is associated with greater bacterial lineage and resistance diversity in wild birds.

title: Genomic tailoring of autogenous poultry vaccines to reduce Campylobacter from farm to fork
authors: Calland JK, Pesonen ME, Mehat J, Pascoe B, et al.
summary: Population genomics used to improve the match between vaccine design and Campylobacter diversity in poultry.

title: Epistasis, core-genome disharmony, and adaptation in recombining bacteria
summary: How interactions across the core genome constrain and enable adaptation in recombining bacterial populations.

title: Host ecology regulates interspecies recombination in bacteria of the genus Campylobacter
summary: Genetic exchange between species is linked to the ecological settings in which bacterial populations meet.
